fresh_facts.claim: US President Donald Trump set a deadline of 8 PM Eastern Time on April 8, 2026, threatening massive strikes on Iran if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.
Iran's media reported attacks on Kharg Island, bridges, and railway tracks as of April 8, 2026.
Iran rejected a US ceasefire proposal on April 6, 2026, to pause the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

US President Trump announced productive phone talks with a top Iranian official led by envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, instructing the Pentagon to delay attacks on Iranian energy infrastructure for at least five days.
Iran received a 15-point US peace plan via Pakistan covering Strait of Hormuz reopening, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated Tehran is reviewing the US peace plan but not negotiating.
A senior Iranian official insisted the war ends only on Iran's terms with end to all-front fighting and no-attack guarantees.
Iran released 20 cargo ships through the Strait of Hormuz starting Monday as of April 8, 2026.
Iran's parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied that US talks occurred and that he would lead negotiations.
Trump reiterated ultimatums threatening to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges if the Strait of Hormuz is not fully reopened by Tuesday.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died in late February 2026 in US-Israel strikes.
Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed Supreme Leader after Ali Khamenei's death and issued a rare statement demanding adversaries' security removal.
President Masoud Pezeshkian accused IRGC commanders of wrecking ceasefire chances via unilateral actions.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr was appointed Supreme National Security Council chief, replacing killed Ali Larijani.
Hardliners attacked ex-Foreign Minister Zarif as a traitor for calling to end the war and urged his arrest along with ex-President Rouhani's.
Pre-Trump deadline strikes killed 49 civilians and injured 58 according to HRANA reporting on Monday.
The Iranian regime executed at least 5 men last week on protest and security charges, including a fourth victim from a Basij base fire.
The Iranian regime denied the body of executed 18-year-old protester Amirhossein Hatami to his family.
Israel accelerated strikes on Tehran airports, aircraft, and targeted IRGC intelligence chief Seyed Majid Khademi.
The US rescued an F-15E crew member during operations in the Iran conflict.
Iran's military spokesperson warned adversaries are unsafe in global tourist centers.
India, Turkey, Egypt, and Pakistan welcomed a ceasefire and backed talks for Strait of Hormuz access.
French President Macron noted that 15 countries are restoring traffic to Iran.
Internet shutdown and regime repression of Iranians remain ongoing as of April 8, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Day 42 escalation has intensified with Trump issuing an 8 PM EDT April 8 deadline for Strait of Hormuz reopening while Israel accelerates strikes on Tehran airports and targeting senior IRGC officials; Iran's regime exhibits severe internal fracture with Pezeshkian accusing IRGC of sabotaging ceasefire, hardliners purging reformists, and contradictory signals including limited ship releases and formal ceasefire rejection despite reviewing a 15-point US peace plan.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump set 8 PM Eastern Time deadline on April 8, 2026, threatening massive strikes if Strait of Hormuz not reopened, while Israel simultaneously accelerated strikes on Tehran airports and targeted IRGC intelligence chief Seyed Majid Khademi.
